sourcesContent:
import * as React from 'react'; import { Link, useParams } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Clock, BookOpen, Users, Star, Play, Award, Target, User, Building, FileText, Calendar, Shield, Scale, Eye, Lock, AlertTriangle, Key, Camera } from 'lucide-react'; const ScenarioDetail: React.FC = () => { const { id } = useParams<{ id: string }>(); // Comprehensive mysterious scenario data based on the PDF content const scenario = { _id: '1', title: 'The Inventory That Changed Everything', subtitle: 'First-Time Bail Application – Theft Offense', description: 'A warehouse supervisor is accused of theft after valuable electronics go missing. As his defense attorney, you must investigate the case, interview the client, research relevant laws, and argue for bail. The truth is hidden in the details - can you uncover what really happened?', caseOverview: `Case File: The Inventory Mystery CLIENT PROFILE: Rajesh Kumar, 32 Warehouse Supervisor at TechDistributors Ltd. Family man with a wife and young daughter Clean criminal record with 5 years of impeccable service Known for his meticulous attention to detail THE INCIDENT: On the morning of October 15, 2025, Rajesh arrived at work as usual for his monthly inventory check at TechDistributors Ltd., a mid-sized electronics distributor in Basavanagudi, Bangalore. The warehouse contained high-end smartphones worth crores of rupees. Following standard procedure, Rajesh and his team of four assistants began scanning barcodes and cross-referencing with digital records. By 11:30 AM, they had completed the process. That's when the nightmare began. Rajesh discovered a discrepancy - 50 premium smartphones valued at ₹2,50,000 were missing from the records. His manager, Mr. Suresh, was called immediately. Within an hour, police arrived and took Rajesh into custody. THE ALLEGATIONS: Mr. Suresh claims that security footage clearly shows Rajesh as the last person in the warehouse the previous evening at 7:30 PM. With no other explanation for the missing inventory, Rajesh has been charged with theft. However, Rajesh maintains his innocence. He insists he followed all proper procedures and locked the warehouse correctly. He believes someone else accessed the warehouse after he left. THE MYSTERY DEEPENS: During your preliminary investigation, you've uncovered several troubling details: 1. SECURITY FOOTAGE GAP: While Rajesh is seen leaving at 7:30 PM, there's a mysterious 2-hour gap in the security recording from 9:00 PM to 11:00 PM. 2. THE MISSING KEY: Rajesh claims there are only two keys to the warehouse - one he carries and one kept by Mr. Suresh. But Mr. Suresh has been unusually evasive when questioned about his key. 3. SUSPICIOUS BEHAVIOR: Rajesh mentioned that one of his assistants, Ravi, had been acting strangely lately. Ravi has been staying late and was reprimanded recently for repeated tardiness. 4. FINANCIAL TROUBLES: Further investigation reveals that Ravi has been seen at a casino frequently, raising questions about possible gambling debts. 5. UNEXPLAINED ACCESS: Warehouse logs show multiple instances of after-hours access that Rajesh cannot account for, but Mr. Suresh refuses to provide details. THE STAKES: Rajesh's bail hearing is scheduled for tomorrow morning. If denied bail, he will remain in custody while the investigation continues. His family is distraught, and his reputation hangs in the balance. YOUR ROLE: As Rajesh's defense attorney, you must: 1. Conduct a thorough client interview to gather all possible facts 2. Investigate alternative explanations for the missing inventory 3. Research relevant bail laws and precedents 4. Prepare compelling arguments for the bail hearing 5. Uncover the truth behind the missing phones KEY SUSPECTS AND WITNESSES: 1. RAJESH KUMAR (Client) - The accused warehouse supervisor 2. MR. SURESH (Manager) - Rajesh's immediate supervisor with access to the warehouse 3. RAVI (Assistant) - Rajesh's subordinate with suspicious behavior 4. PRIYA KUMAR (Wife) - Rajesh's wife who may have relevant information 5. SECURITY GUARD - On duty during the relevant time period EVIDENCE TO EXAMINE: - Security camera footage and timeline analysis - Warehouse key access records - Employee financial records and background - Physical evidence at the scene - Witness testimonies and credibility assessment - Company policies and procedures documentation The evidence is there - you just need to find it. But be warned, not everyone may be telling the truth, and the real perpetrator may be hiding in plain sight.`, practiceArea: 'Criminal Law', difficulty: 'Intermediate', estimatedTime: 30, rating: 4.7, reviewCount: 32, price: 0, // Free scenario tags: ['bail application', 'client interviewing', 'legal research', 'oral advocacy', 'investigation', 'evidence analysis', 'mystery'], status: 'published', createdAt: '2025-01-15T10:30:00Z', updatedAt: '2025-01-20T14:45:00Z', creator: { username: 'LegalExpert2025', name: 'Legal Training Institute' }, learningObjectives: [ 'Conduct an effective client interview to gather critical facts', 'Analyze evidence and identify gaps in the prosecution\'s case', 'Research relevant bail laws and precedents under CrPC', 'Develop critical thinking and legal reasoning skills', 'Prepare compelling arguments for bail grant', 'Present oral arguments in a mock bail hearing', 'Understand the importance of thorough investigation in building a defense' ], courtType: 'Magistrate Court', parties: [ { role: 'Defendant', name: 'Rajesh Kumar', description: 'Warehouse supervisor accused of theft. Claims innocence and proper procedure.' }, { role: 'Defense Attorney', name: 'You', description: 'Assigned to represent the defendant and uncover the truth.' }, { role: 'Prosecutor', name: 'State Prosecutor', description: 'Representing the state with circumstantial evidence against Rajesh.' }, { role: 'Key Witness', name: 'Mr. Suresh', description: 'Warehouse manager who reported the incident. Possesses the second warehouse key.' }, { role: 'Person of Interest', name: 'Ravi', description: 'Assistant with access to the warehouse. Recently reprimanded and seen at casino.' }, { role: 'Supporting Witness', name: 'Priya Kumar', description: 'Rajesh\'s wife who may have relevant information about his activities.' } ], skillFocus: [ 'Client Interviewing', 'Legal Research', 'Evidence Analysis', 'Oral Advocacy', 'Case Preparation', 'Critical Thinking', 'Investigation Techniques' ], duration: '20-30 minutes', assessmentCriteria: [ 'Thoroughness of client interview', 'Quality of legal research', 'Identification of evidence gaps', 'Strength of arguments presented', 'Oral advocacy skills', 'Professional courtroom demeanor', 'Investigative approach' ], investigationPoints: [ 'Security camera footage analysis', 'Warehouse key access records', 'Employee background checks', 'Financial records of suspects', 'Physical evidence at the scene', 'Witness credibility assessment', 'Company policy compliance', 'Timeline reconstruction' ], legalIssues: [ 'Bail application procedures under CrPC', 'Burden of proof in criminal cases', 'Circumstantial evidence evaluation', 'Right to legal representation', 'Presumption of innocence', 'Evidence admissibility standards' ], evidenceToExamine: [ 'Security footage from multiple cameras', 'Warehouse access log books', 'Financial transaction records', 'Employee disciplinary files', 'Maintenance records for security systems', 'Witness statements and alibis' ], suspiciousActivities: [ '2-hour gap in security footage', 'Mr. Suresh\'s evasiveness about his key', 'Ravi\'s recent behavioral changes', 'Frequent casino visits by Ravi', 'Unexplained after-hours warehouse access', 'Mr. Suresh\'s defensive responses to questions' ] }; const getDifficultyIcon = (difficulty: string) => { switch (difficulty) { case 'Beginner': return '🌱'; case 'Intermediate': return '📊'; case 'Advanced': return '🔥'; case 'Expert': return '👑'; default: return '📚'; } }; return (

); }; export default ScenarioDetail;
